title: Homer At The Bat

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, culturally iconic

jokes.text: Mmm, doughnuts.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying food
jokes.punchline: His characteristic food appreciation noise
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food appreciation - character-specific and economical

jokes.text: Don't tell me how to eat doughnuts.
jokes.setup: Someone warns Homer about choking while eating
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive response about his donut-eating expertise
jokes.explanation: Homer treating donut consumption as a skill demonstrates his character perfectly

jokes.text: That's for the hiccups.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests scaring Homer to help with choking
jokes.punchline: Correcting that scaring is for hiccups, not choking
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about mixed-up home remedies

jokes.text: Last year we were 2 and 28. It wasn't our best season. Actually, it was.
jokes.setup: Admitting their terrible 2-28 record wasn't their best season
jokes.punchline: Revealing it actually WAS their best season
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke - first admits failure, then makes it worse

jokes.text: Hmm.
jokes.setup: Homer considers joining the team after hearing about a secret weapon
jokes.punchline: Homer's thoughtful consideration sound
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat showing Homer's decision-making process

jokes.text: What's that? A homemade bat? It's something very special-- a homemade bat.
jokes.setup: Bart asks what Homer is showing him
jokes.punchline: Homer's redundant explanation of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Homer's redundant explanation style is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Sheltering myself with a large piece of sheet metal... I ran for cover under the tallest tree I could find.
jokes.setup: Homer tells his lightning story
jokes.punchline: Using metal for shelter then standing under the tallest tree - both terrible ideas for lightning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - doing exactly the wrong things for lightning safety

jokes.text: I put my homemade football on hold
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his woodworking priorities
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of making a homemade football
jokes.explanation: Absurd throwaway detail about Homer's other DIY sports equipment projects

jokes.text: Homie, come to bed. Homer, go back to the garage.
jokes.setup: Marge calling Homer to bed
jokes.punchline: Immediately reversing and sending him back to the garage
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Marge's changing her mind about Homer's project

jokes.text: We play 30 games, 10 at bats a game-- 3,000.
jokes.setup: Bart asks how many home runs Homer will hit
jokes.punchline: Homer's impossible math suggesting he'll hit a home run every at-bat
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional confidence and bad math are perfectly in character

jokes.text: You can't leave first until you chug a beer. Any man scoring has to chug a beer. Chug a beer at the top of all odd-numbered innings. And the fourth inning is the beer inning.
jokes.setup: Explaining softball ground rules
jokes.punchline: Every rule involves drinking beer
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of beer rules, getting more ridiculous with each addition

jokes.text: Hey, we know how to play softball.
jokes.setup: After hearing all the beer-drinking rules
jokes.punchline: Deadpan response that they know the 'rules'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to the absurd beer rules

jokes.text: And there he goes off in that direction. And everyone is happy.
jokes.setup: Marge attempting to call the game
jokes.punchline: Her extremely basic, non-technical commentary
jokes.explanation: Marge's innocent, non-sports commentary style is endearing and funny

jokes.text: Yeah, I got a magic bat too. And I got an enchanted jockstrap.
jokes.setup: Homer shows off his magic bat
jokes.punchline: Coworkers sarcastically claiming their own magical equipment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from magic bat to enchanted jockstrap - absurd but logical progression

jokes.text: Ha-ha! Cops can't win!
jokes.setup: Police team loses to power plant team
jokes.punchline: Barney's gleeful taunt about cops losing
jokes.explanation: Barney's anti-authority attitude comes through in this simple taunt

jokes.text: I got my magic bat off a piano. My sister let me use her wooden leg.
jokes.setup: Everyone claiming to have special bats
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous sources for bats
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of bat sources, wooden leg is particularly dark and funny

jokes.text: I got my magic bat off a piano. - My sister let me use her wooden leg.
jokes.setup: Coworkers trying to replicate Homer's success
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd sources for their 'magic' bats
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from piano to wooden leg - second line is genuinely surprising

jokes.text: The Gladiators from my power plant will crush your team like nine flabby grapes.
jokes.setup: Burns trash-talking the opposing team
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and poetic insult about 'flabby grapes'
jokes.explanation: Burns' peculiar aristocratic way of insulting - 'flabby grapes' is memorably weird

jokes.text: Why don't we make it interesting? What, a million dollars isn't interesting?
jokes.setup: Competitor suggests making the bet interesting
jokes.punchline: Burns' confusion since a million dollars is already huge
jokes.explanation: Burns' wealthy perspective makes a million seem small - good character comedy

jokes.text: No, sir. Who would you like killed?
jokes.setup: Burns asks if he should cheat
jokes.punchline: Smithers immediately offering murder as an option
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers moment - casually offering extreme solutions with perfect economy

jokes.text: Honus Wagner, Cap Anson... Mordecai Brown-- Uh, sir... I'm afraid all those players have retired... and, uh, passed on. In fact, your right fielder has been dead for 130 years.
jokes.setup: Burns naming old baseball players as ringers
jokes.punchline: Smithers explaining they're all dead, especially the 130-year-dead one
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns age joke - he's so old he thinks dead players are current, escalates to 130 years

jokes.text: Damnation!
jokes.setup: Learning his chosen players are all dead
jokes.punchline: Burns' old-fashioned curse word
jokes.explanation: Burns' antiquated vocabulary shines through in his frustration

jokes.text: the American League, the National League... the Negro leagues.
jokes.setup: Burns listing baseball leagues to scout
jokes.punchline: Including the Negro leagues as if they're still separate/current
jokes.explanation: Shows Burns' age and disconnect from modern times, though dated humor

jokes.text: It's a pay cut, but what the hey.
jokes.setup: Canseco offered $50,000 for one game
jokes.punchline: Calling it a pay cut from his MLB salary
jokes.explanation: Good joke about professional athlete salaries being so high that $50K is a cut

jokes.text: Hey, sorry. I thought you were a deer.
jokes.setup: Smithers recruiting players
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning he mistook someone for a deer
jokes.explanation: Absurd throwaway line that's both random and oddly specific

jokes.text: It's okay... but sometimes I wish I had something more blue collar... with big machines and cool dials and stuff... like an oil refinery... or hydroelectric plant.
jokes.setup: Asked about playing for the Dodgers
jokes.punchline: Preferring dangerous industrial work over professional baseball
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - professional athlete wanting dangerous blue-collar work

jokes.text: One more outburst, and I'll send you back to the big leagues.
jokes.setup: Scioscia complaining about his job
jokes.punchline: Burns threatening to send him back to his prestigious MLB career
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal joke - treating MLB career as punishment rather than reward

jokes.text: Well, I've never met you, but... yes.
jokes.setup: Barney asks if Strawberry is better than him at baseball
jokes.punchline: Strawberry's polite but honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly polite but brutally honest response from a professional athlete

jokes.text: Now that you're older, I can tell you that's a crock. No matter how good you are... there's always a million people better. Gotcha-- Can't win, don't try.
jokes.setup: Homer admitting he lied about Bart being able to do anything
jokes.punchline: Homer's nihilistic life lesson and Bart's takeaway
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Homer destroying motivational parenting with brutal honesty

jokes.text: Gotcha-- Can't win, don't try.
jokes.setup: Homer's pessimistic life lesson about always being outmatched
jokes.punchline: Bart's succinct summary of the terrible advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect distillation of Homer's awful advice into a memorable catchphrase.

jokes.text: Homer Simpson, sir.
jokes.setup: Burns asks for an example of a bad player
jokes.punchline: Smithers immediately naming Homer
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline with Smithers knowing exactly who the worst player is

jokes.text: It's like there's a party in my mouth... and everyone's invited.
jokes.setup: Tasting Burns' brain and nerve tonic
jokes.punchline: Overly enthusiastic food advertisement-style description
jokes.explanation: Memorable quote parodying food advertisement enthusiasm, became culturally quotable

jokes.text: That's impossible. No one can give more than 100%. By definition, that is the most anyone can give.
jokes.setup: Burns demanding 110% effort
jokes.punchline: Mattingly's logical response about mathematical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the logical flaw in demanding over 100%

jokes.text: There's no 'I' in 'team.' Some of these guys have a bad attitude, Skip. They sure do, Strawberry.
jokes.setup: Burns using sports clichés
jokes.punchline: Strawberry agreeing with criticism of his own teammates
jokes.explanation: Good sports cliché parody with Strawberry being a suck-up to management

jokes.text: Mattingly, get rid of those sideburns. What sideburns? You heard me, hippie.
jokes.setup: Burns demanding Mattingly shave sideburns
jokes.punchline: Mattingly having no sideburns, Burns calling him a hippie anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic absurd Burns moment - obsessing over non-existent sideburns, became iconic

jokes.text: You sure did. I did? Whoo-hoo! In your face, Strawberry.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if he made the team
jokes.punchline: Homer's celebration and trash talk to a superstar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - celebrating making a team he was already on, taunting a hall-of-famer

jokes.text: unless, of course, my nine all-stars... fall victim to nine separate misfortunes... and are unable to play tomorrow. But that will never happen.
jokes.setup: Burns confident about winning
jokes.punchline: Describing exactly what will happen while saying it won't
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - Burns describes exactly what will happen while dismissing it

jokes.text: Well, well. Steve Sax from New York City. I heard some guy got killed in New York... and they never solved the case. But you wouldn't know anything about that... now, would you, Steve?
jokes.setup: Police stop Steve Sax
jokes.punchline: Immediately accusing him of random NYC murder based solely on being from New York
jokes.explanation: Absurd police logic - pinning unsolved murders on anyone from NYC

jokes.text: But there's hundreds of unsolved murders... in New York City. You don't know when to keep your mouth shut... do you, Saxy boy?
jokes.setup: Sax logically pointing out NYC has many unsolved murders
jokes.punchline: Officer treating this reasonable response as suspicious
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - reasonable response makes him look more guilty to illogical cops

jokes.text: Can't... lift... arm... or... speak... at... normal... rate.
jokes.setup: Scioscia suffering from radiation poisoning
jokes.punchline: Speaking slowly while describing speaking slowly
jokes.explanation: Self-demonstrating joke where the delivery matches the content

jokes.text: No. By tomorrow... you'll barely be able to breathe. Oh... man.
jokes.setup: Doctor's grim prognosis about radiation poisoning
jokes.punchline: Scioscia's slow, defeated response
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about radiation poisoning symptoms, delivered in character's affected speech

jokes.text: Good Lord! Gigantism!
jokes.setup: Ken Griffey Jr. overdoses on nerve tonic
jokes.punchline: Doctor's dramatic diagnosis of gigantism (Griffey's jaw is huge)
jokes.explanation: Visual gag setup with dramatic medical diagnosis, Griffey's swollen jaw is the visual payoff

jokes.text: Good Lord! Gigantism!
jokes.setup: Ken Griffey Jr. has been taking nerve tonic
jokes.punchline: The tonic has caused his head to swell enormously
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to Burns' earlier warning about the tonic causing gigantism.

jokes.text: My player piano! - Oh, man.
jokes.setup: Canseco heroically saving cats and babies
jokes.punchline: Woman's ridiculous request to save a player piano, Canseco's exasperated response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from life-saving to saving a piano - absurd priorities revealed

jokes.text: Oh, man.
jokes.setup: Canseco keeps getting distracted by rescuing people/things
jokes.punchline: His frustrated reaction to missing the game for a player piano
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Canseco's do-gooder nature leading to his downfall

jokes.text: England's greatest prime minister... was Lord Palmerston! Pitt, the Elder!
jokes.setup: Bar argument about English prime ministers
jokes.punchline: Baseball player and drunk arguing about obscure historical figures
jokes.explanation: Absurd bar fight topic - historically obscure and completely unrelated to baseball

jokes.text: You watch too many movies, Sax.
jokes.setup: Sax asking for his lawyer
jokes.punchline: Officer dismissing basic legal rights as movie fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on police procedure vs. legal rights portrayed in media

jokes.text: Wow! One, please.
jokes.setup: Ozzie approaches the Mystery Spot attraction despite warnings
jokes.punchline: His excited response to potential danger
jokes.explanation: Simple enthusiasm in the face of obvious danger.

jokes.text: We've seen it, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer not wanting family to see him on the bench
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact confirmation they've already seen his butt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart deadpan - literal interpretation of Homer's metaphor about his 'worthless butt'

jokes.text: For the last time, get rid of those sideburns. I don't know what you think sideburns are, but-- Don't argue with me! Just get rid of them!
jokes.setup: Burns continuing to demand Mattingly remove non-existent sideburns
jokes.punchline: Mattingly's confusion and Burns' refusal to listen
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the sideburns running gag, Burns getting more irrational

jokes.text: That damn hypnotist! My starting pitcher thinks he's a chicken.
jokes.setup: Explaining what happened to Roger Clemens
jokes.punchline: Star pitcher hypnotized into thinking he's a chicken
jokes.explanation: Classic hypnotist trope applied to professional athlete, absurd but logical setup

jokes.text: I'm going to report this... to the American Hypnotical Association.
jokes.setup: Burns angry about the hypnotist
jokes.punchline: Threatening to report to made-up 'Hypnotical Association'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'Hypnotical' instead of 'Hypnotic', shows Burns' vocabulary quirks

jokes.text: Mattingly! I told you to trim those sideburns. Go home! You're off the team! For good!
jokes.setup: Burns still obsessed with Mattingly's non-existent sideburns
jokes.punchline: Finally kicking him off the team for imaginary grooming violation
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the running gag - Burns' obsession costs him a Hall of Fame player

jokes.text: I still like him better than Steinbrenner.
jokes.setup: Mattingly getting kicked off for imaginary sideburns
jokes.punchline: Preferring crazy Burns to his real Yankees owner
jokes.explanation: Excellent real-world reference - Steinbrenner was notoriously difficult, making Burns seem reasonable

jokes.text: Mom, they're professional athletes. They're used to this. It rolls right off their backs.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting that booing isn't nice
jokes.punchline: Lisa's explanation followed by Strawberry visibly upset
jokes.explanation: Good setup for visual gag of Strawberry clearly affected by the booing

jokes.text: Children, tell me... when your father stops scratching himself.
jokes.setup: Homer has some kind of reaction/condition
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact request for kids to monitor Homer's scratching
jokes.explanation: Marge's practical approach to Homer's gross behavior, treating it as routine

jokes.text: Mmm... potato chips.
jokes.setup: Homer daydreaming during important game situation
jokes.punchline: His characteristic food fantasy noise
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food fantasy during crucial moment, callback to earlier food appreciation

jokes.text: No, Mom. It counts as a hit. Dad just won the game.
jokes.setup: Homer gets knocked unconscious by the baseball
jokes.punchline: Lisa explaining that being hit by the pitch counts as winning
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending - Homer wins by getting hurt, ultimate anti-hero sports moment

jokes.text: Mike Scioscia's tragic illness made us smile
jokes.setup: Recap song celebrating their victory
jokes.punchline: Admitting they found someone's radiation poisoning amusing
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny admission that they enjoyed someone's suffering because it helped them win.

tmdb_overview: Burns hires Major League ringers for the plant softball championship.
insight: Baseball guest stars drive 60 jokes in 22 minutes—peak early Simpsons density.
